methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring

data.narrative:
Oxford Nanopore Technologies is a public-stage biotech & health company that has raised over $1B to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Tempus AI ($6.0B), Freenome ($2.6B), Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.8B), and Alto Neuroscience ($529M).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across these 4 peers is $2.2B, with a range spanning $529M to $6.0B. This dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in the biotech public market—particularly the 11x spread between the lowest and highest peer—but the median anchors a defensible central estimate. A public-comp anchoring approach using price-to-sales multiples (sector median: 7.8x across 3 public tickers) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue for the target, a common constraint with private-stage companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.9B (low) to $2.5B (high), with a median point estimate of $2.2B. This analysis rests on a single applicable method given data constraints. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer cohort—particularly whether Tempus AI's $6.0B valuation reflects comparable technical risk and revenue maturity—and the absence of revenue metrics to anchor multiples-based cross-checks. The wide peer range suggests material valuation dispersion within the sector.
